candidates.placement: Commons power, benefit, harm, and accountability relations
candidates.summary: Role and authority identify the entrusted position. Deception or disproportionate benefit can show how it is used; exclusion, coercion, or retaliation shows who bears the effect. Concealed accountability belongs in the same account. These clauses make institutional harm criticisable without one moral label hiding its mechanism, while an exact source framework can remain beside them.

candidates.concept: cooperatives, federations, unions, mutual aid, public protest, strikes, and civil disobedience
candidates.placement: exact institutional or movement tradition beside collective Phi action
candidates.summary: Phi can describe cooperation and alliance, shared institutions and resources, or advocacy and objection. Collective refusal and work that ceases remain direct acts. These relations support nonviolent collective action without pretending that one generic label exhausts the history or commitments of a cooperative, union, protest, strike, or civil-disobedience tradition.
candidates.source_rule: Named institutional forms and movement identities retain their own names and attributed meanings. Organising traditions, protest forms, labour actions, and declarations stay with them. Community-specific mutual aid keeps its source as well. Phi can describe participants and purpose, the action and claimed authority, then risk, refusal, or response around them.

candidates.concept: household membership, co-residence, roommates, and domestic roles
candidates.placement: Household and Commons relations
candidates.summary: `womuri` identifies the domestic unit, and `menui` states who dwells where. Membership and role enter only when people actually claim them. A roommate or co-resident can therefore be described through shared dwelling without turning proximity into kinship, authority, or assigned work.

candidates.concept: hospitality as a relation among guest, host, welcome, and care
candidates.placement: Household guest and welcome relations
candidates.summary: `phemi` and `phemila` name the two temporary positions, while `whelani` says that an arrival is wanted. Food, shelter, privacy, and care remain concrete acts around them. The composition leaves each household or tradition free to decide what hospitality asks of either person.

candidates.concept: tidying, putting away, and household routine
candidates.placement: base placement, storage, cleanliness, and habitual-action clauses
candidates.summary: Tidying is the work of putting particular things in their intended places, storing what waits for later, and cleaning what needs it. Phi states those changes directly. Habitual `ro` can show a household routine without turning every repeated domestic act into one opaque category.

candidates.concept: food storage, cooling, drying, salting, and preservation
candidates.placement: base preservation relations with Household containers and food conditions
candidates.summary: Storage says where food waits for retrieval; preservation attends to keeping its condition. Dryness and temperature can enter alongside salt and closure, while later spoilage remains observable. The same relations suit cellars and jars as readily as drying racks or cold stores. Powered refrigeration does not become the universal household answer.

candidates.concept: frying, roasting, steaming, stirring, kneading, serving, and other food preparation methods
candidates.placement: base and Household preparation verbs beside exact culinary sources
candidates.summary: General cooking and preparation already combine with a heat source or vessel. Liquid and movement can be stated when they matter; cutting, grinding, and material change stay visible too. `napako` and `therapi` keep baking and boiling direct where those physical methods recur. A named culinary method can retain its source identity when the tradition or exact technique matters.

candidates.concept: housework, chores, assignment, workload, drudgery, and domestic power
candidates.placement: Household, Work, Commons, Accessibility, and base relations
candidates.summary: A chore remains a concrete `riporu` task attached to the work itself. Washing and cooking remain distinct from cleaning, repair, or care. Assignment and acceptance stay separate, as do workload and drudgery. Coercion, exploitation, or retaliation can be named when domestic arrangements use power harmfully instead of hiding that relation inside a softer household word.

candidates.concept: exact sanitation, cleaning, food-safety, and domestic health instructions
candidates.placement: exact health, product, infrastructure, or community source beside a Household account
candidates.summary: Phi can describe dirt and cleanliness, washing and soap, and the material path through a latrine. It can also name contamination, spoilage, or infection and the people affected. A threshold or prescribed procedure still depends on the authority and method that established it.
candidates.source_rule: Cleaning-product identities and warnings retain their source form. Sanitation designs and maintenance instructions do as well. Food-safety rules retain their contaminant limits, public-health authority, and clinical guidance. Phi can discuss the material path and observed condition around that record. Access, action, and response remain available without reconstructing the source from a household paraphrase.

candidates.concept: household utilities, service accounts, meter records, and named appliances
candidates.placement: exact service, billing, equipment, or technical source beside Household and Systems relations
candidates.summary: Water and heat remain discussable in Phi, as do electricity and supply. Storage has its own relation. Service loss, faults, and repair can then be stated around the exact device or service identity. Source measurements and account records stay exact.
candidates.source_rule: Provider and service names retain their source form. Account identifiers and meter readings do too, alongside tariffs and bills. Equipment models keep their ratings, controls, and repair instructions. Phi can identify who uses or supplies the service and what stopped working. The burden and proposed response remain available around those exact records.

candidates.concept: addresses, residence status, tenancy, leases, rent, and domestic legal claims
candidates.placement: exact place, legal, community, or economic source beside Household and Commons relations
candidates.summary: Phi can state who dwells in a place and who claims ownership or authority there. Obligations and memberships remain separate relations. They do not recreate an address or legal status, and Phi's refusal of monetary vocabulary leaves rent and payment records in their exact source form.
candidates.source_rule: Addresses retain their original script and formatting. Residence and tenancy categories remain exact beside leases and notices. Property records retain rent amounts, payment histories, and jurisdiction-specific rights. Phi can discuss consent and access around them, or coercion and burden. Appeal and redress remain available without replacing the operative wording.

candidates.concept: wounds, bleeding, bruising, burns, fractures, sprains, dressings, and ordinary first aid
candidates.placement: base body and material vocabulary with Medical injury and care
candidates.summary: The useful account says what changed in the body and what care followed. A cut or burn names the event, flowing blood names bleeding, and a broken bone or swollen joint names the affected structure. Cloth can cover an injury without making one culturally specific dressing the universal form of first aid.

candidates.concept: diarrhoea, constipation, urinary frequency, retention, and incontinence
candidates.placement: base bodily substances, release, recurrence, possibility, and condition descriptions
candidates.summary: Ordinary care can name faeces or urine, describe its form, and say how often release occurs or whether the person can control it. Exact clinical categories use different duration and frequency criteria, so the source classification remains beside the person's more direct report.

candidates.concept: rash, bruising, swelling, discharge, and other visible or felt surface changes
candidates.placement: base skin, colour, substance, sensation, and change vocabulary with Medical findings
candidates.summary: A broad surface label would lose the features that make the report useful. Phi can locate the skin change and describe its colour or extent. It can then add whatever actually appears: itch, pain, warmth, swelling, fluid, or a finding from an examination.

candidates.concept: fainting, seizure, collapse, and other sudden changes in consciousness or control
candidates.placement: base or Medical according to emergency and lived-experience scenarios
candidates.summary: Base `waeli` distinguishes conscious experience, and ordinary clauses can report loss of awareness or bodily control. A direct seizure or fainting word may still earn its place because a witness often needs it quickly, but the terms must not collapse distinct events or imply a diagnosis.
candidates.revisit_when: emergency, chronic-care, or first-person scenarios repeatedly need a stable event beyond a direct account of awareness, movement, breathing, and recovery

candidates.concept: pulse, respiratory rate, blood pressure, oxygen saturation, and exact vital-sign records
candidates.placement: Medical description beside the exact clinical measurement record
candidates.summary: Heart rhythm and breathing can be observed in Phi, and `sumeri` names temperature as a parameter. A new vital-sign word would still not make the measurement clinically usable: that depends on how, where, and when it was taken. The record therefore stays exact.
candidates.source_rule: The clinical record retains the exact parameter name and reading, together with the method that produced it. Device, site, time, reference range, and source interpretation stay with that record. Phi may describe the person's experience, the observed rhythm or change, and how the record enters a care discussion.

candidates.concept: examination, checkup, observation, monitoring, testing, reassessment, and follow-up
candidates.placement: base and module evidence, assessment, recurrence, plan, and schedule vocabulary
candidates.summary: Observation attends to what is present, a test runs a bounded procedure, and assessment compares evidence with a criterion. Monitoring repeats observation through time. Follow-up returns to the person or matter under a plan, which keeps the promised action more visible than one broad clinical verb would.

candidates.concept: clinic, hospital, appointment, referral, patient, clinician, nurse, caregiver, and other care roles
candidates.placement: base place and person vocabulary with Commons roles, Work handoff, and exact institutional titles
candidates.summary: A care place can be described by what happens there, and an appointment is a scheduled meeting or care action. Referral identifies who directs a person or record towards another role, while handoff follows the transfer itself. Patient and professional titles stay apart from personhood, consent, qualification, and authority.

candidates.concept: named procedures, operations, surgery, therapy, rehabilitation methods, and technical intervention classes
candidates.placement: exact clinical source beside Medical intervention and treatment vocabulary
candidates.summary: `thekamu` names a deliberate health intervention and `nephoru` a treatment aim. A broad root for procedure would hide the details that distinguish one technique from another. Its technical identity therefore stays with the source.
candidates.source_rule: The source retains the exact procedure or therapy name, its version or technique, and the affected site. Purpose, risks, alternatives, practitioner, and follow-up stay with it. Phi can discuss the proposal and expected effect, the person's questions and decision, then what happened afterwards.

candidates.concept: urgency, emergency, first response, ambulance, and immediate care
candidates.placement: base danger, time, necessity, care, communication, and Medical triage relations
candidates.summary: Urgency joins possible harm to the time available for action; severity alone cannot supply it. A direct account can say what is happening, who needs care, and what must happen next. Triage enters only when constrained capacity requires an order, while an exact emergency category or service name stays with its institution.

candidates.concept: prevention, infection control, protective precautions, and public-health support
candidates.placement: base protection and care with Medical transmission, exposure, monitoring, and review
candidates.summary: Prevention begins with the possible harm and proposed protective act. Infection control adds the suspected agent and pathway, then the expected effect and practical burden. Consent, authority, material support, and review remain audible instead of disappearing inside a protective label.

candidates.concept: impairment, disability, access need, assistance, capacity, and participation
candidates.placement: Medical and Accessibility relations with base self-report and consent
candidates.summary: `murethi` describes a functional difference or limitation, while `sowelu` follows disability in relation to barriers and participation. Reachability, use, assistance, and support answer further questions. Medical classification therefore cannot decide whether someone is disabled or what participation they want.

candidates.concept: chronic care, supportive care, palliation, rehabilitation, home care, and end-of-life care
candidates.placement: base care and comfort with Medical treatment, recovery, course, plan, and exact source terminology
candidates.summary: Long care becomes clearer when the account says whether its present work seeks change, comfort, adaptation, rest, or practical support. Recovery may remain one person's aim and not another's. Exact programme and intervention names stay with their source, particularly when they carry eligibility or institutional promises.

candidates.concept: cure, remission, recurrence, deterioration, improvement, and treatment response
candidates.placement: base change and healing with Medical recovery, relapse, symptom, finding, and course vocabulary
candidates.summary: Cure makes a stronger claim than treatment or present improvement, so a speaker states which condition ended and under whose criterion. Remission and recurrence likewise depend on a previous state and clinical source. Recovery and relapse keep their direct words; other changes identify what improved or worsened and over which interval.

candidates.concept: exact disease names, diagnostic criteria, case definitions, classifications, public-health categories, and reporting rules
candidates.placement: medical, scientific, institutional, or community source beside a Phi health account
candidates.summary: Phi can discuss a disease, diagnosis, outbreak, or rule and examine its evidence. The exact category still determines who is counted, what follows, and which authority made the claim. Translating it approximately would change the record precisely where the distinction matters.
candidates.source_rule: Disease and syndrome names retain their source form with diagnostic criteria and authorship. Case definitions, severity classes, and public-health categories keep their population, place, dates, thresholds, and issuing source. Reporting duties and legal instructions remain exact beside the Phi discussion of effect, consent, access, and review.

candidates.concept: access requirement, access need, requested change, and accommodation
candidates.placement: Accessibility access, need, request, choice, and accommodation clauses
candidates.summary: An access need belongs to a person in a particular activity and set of conditions. The speaker states what is required, asks for or chooses a change, and then names the accommodation actually made. Keeping those moments separate prevents a request from being reported as consent or a completed result.

candidates.concept: assistive device or technology, mobility aid, service animal, and support person
candidates.placement: Accessibility assistance, device, animal, support, and independence relations
candidates.summary: An assistive device is `naphelu keli` when the supporting function matters. An animal or person can likewise be named with the help or support they provide, while `ralemi` keeps direction over the activity with the participant. Exact identities and formal roles stay with their records.

candidates.concept: exact assistive-device models, service-animal designations, support roles, and individual access plans
candidates.placement: device, service, institutional, or personal source beside an Accessibility account
candidates.summary: Phi explains what support a device, animal, person, or plan provides without recoding the identity on which repair, recognition, training, or an agreed arrangement depends.
candidates.source_rule: Device names, models, and technical instructions stay in their source form. The same is true of service-animal designations and training records, formal support roles, and individual access plans. Phi describes present use and practical effect around them, with consent, maintenance, and change still available to the account.

candidates.concept: ramps, lifts, handrails, adapted fixtures, tactile paving, transfer surfaces, and evacuation features
candidates.placement: a connected Built Environment, Accessibility, Systems, Household, or Work pass
candidates.summary: A ramp or lift changes the route and its levels. Handrails, tactile paving, adapted fixtures, transfer surfaces, and evacuation features answer other questions about grip, perception, bodily movement, and emergency passage. The present module can describe their functions, but direct forms need a sustained place-based scenario rather than a catalogue of fixtures.
candidates.revisit_when: a connected built-environment or access scenario repeatedly needs one stable feature beyond a physical description and its exact source identity

candidates.concept: environmental and sensory access conditions
candidates.placement: base sensory and environmental qualities with Accessibility barrier, access, and overload relations
candidates.summary: Noise, illumination, temperature, and odour are first described as conditions. Crowding and sensory load receive the same treatment. `kopharu`, `ruweli`, and `pushali` then show what the setting changes for a particular person or activity, instead of treating one tolerance as the room's natural setting.

candidates.concept: visibility and visual availability
candidates.placement: base perception and light clauses with Accessibility use relations
candidates.summary: Existing `po nila` states that something can be seen. Illumination and contrast conditions can then be named, while `kelasu` or `lokane` asks whether seeing it is enough for the intended use. The earlier completeness review already settled this composition; the module needs no synonym for visible.

candidates.concept: text alternatives, spoken or audio description, plain rephrasing, summaries, and multimodal communication
candidates.placement: Accessibility communication, description, writing, speech, format, and equivalence relations
candidates.summary: A text alternative writes a description in another format; spoken description speaks it. Rephrasing and summary do different work, and `samethu` can state bounded equivalence when that claim is warranted. The parts let a speaker say what information survived the change instead of treating every alternative as interchangeable.

candidates.concept: named signed languages, Braille and AAC systems, plain-language standards, and access conformance regimes
candidates.placement: language, community, technical, or legal source beside an Accessibility account
candidates.summary: A format or communication-mode description does not replace the name and rules of a language, script, system, or standard. The source identity matters to the people using it and to anyone checking whether a promised form was actually supplied.
candidates.source_rule: Signed languages, Braille systems, and augmentative and alternative communication systems keep the names their users and sources supply. Easy Read or plain-language standards, caption and transcript specifications, accessibility standards, versions, and conformance results likewise stay exact. Phi describes their availability and practical effect around that record.

candidates.concept: advance notice, agenda material, processing time, rest, postponement, asynchronous contribution, and predictable timing
candidates.placement: base and Accessibility notice, reading, thought, rest, schedule, and participation clauses
candidates.summary: Notice can arrive before an event, and reading or thought can take time. A pause may be rest or a recess; an event may move; a contribution may arrive asynchronously. Pace, schedule, deadline, and predictability give participants more useful things to negotiate than a general promise of enough time.

candidates.concept: temporary, fluctuating, and situational access needs, including fatigue, pain, stamina, and overload
candidates.placement: base bodily condition, change, time, rest, demand, and Accessibility access relations
candidates.summary: A speaker can report the present bodily or sensory condition, say that it changes, and name the rest, pace, assistance, or altered demand now required. The access relation need not become permanent before it is real, and a changing need need not be squeezed into one fixed identity word.

candidates.concept: emergency access, evacuation, first response, and immediate assistance
candidates.placement: base danger, route, notice, assistance, refusal, and time relations with Accessibility wayfinding
candidates.summary: An emergency account begins with the present danger, the available route, the notice given, and the help offered. `runaki` keeps orientation with the person; consent or refusal still applies to contact and assistance. Exact service names and evacuation plans remain with their sources.

candidates.concept: digital and interface access, alternative input, compatibility, redundant paths, and user testing
candidates.placement: Accessibility and Systems interface, format, device, compatibility, redundancy, reliability, and test relations
candidates.summary: A digital access account can identify the interface and the chosen format or device, then report compatibility, an alternative route, and the observed test result. Exact software, hardware, standards, and test methods remain source material. Accessibility stays a claim about a stated person, purpose, and set of conditions.

candidates.concept: exclusion, discrimination, access denial, complaint, appeal, review, and redress
candidates.placement: Accessibility and Commons harm, exclusion, coercion, complaint, review, and repair relations
candidates.summary: Phi names exclusion, category-shaped harm, coercion, and retaliation, then states the complaint, appeal, review, or repair being sought. The account still says what happened and who bears responsibility for changing it.

candidates.concept: interpreter, captioner, transcriber, support worker, advocate, and representative roles
candidates.placement: person nouns with Accessibility events and Commons role, representation, and authorization relations
candidates.summary: Phi names the person and the work or relation in the current event. Interpretation, captioning, and transcription concern communication; practical support and advocacy do other work. Representation, delegation, and authorization each need their own source, so one helper label cannot quietly grant authority over another person's words or decisions.

candidates.concept: accessible, inclusive, and universal design with consultation and user testing
candidates.placement: Accessibility access, inclusion, design, consultation, equivalence, participation, and test relations
candidates.summary: A design can name the people and activities considered, the consultation that informed it, and the testing that followed. `lokane` and `pharune` describe observed access and inclusion without borrowing a doctrine's universal promise. Exact design standards keep their source wording.

candidates.concept: exact disability, ableism, community, identity, legal, and clinical terminology
candidates.placement: personal, community, legal, clinical, or scholarly source beside an Accessibility account
candidates.summary: Broad Phi words support discussion of disability, impairment, barriers, and participation. The person or relevant source supplies the identity wording and any finer community, legal, clinical, or scholarly distinction.
candidates.source_rule: A person's self-description stays as they give it. Community and source terms keep their names, spelling, capitalization, script, definitions, classifications, and authorship. Phi describes the surrounding access relation without treating its broad vocabulary as a replacement.

candidates.concept: steps, workflow, work sequence, milestones, completion, and work status
candidates.placement: Work tasks, dependencies, stages, criteria, and status words
candidates.summary: A workflow is the order among actual tasks and the dependencies joining them. `nuwoe` marks a stage, while progress, pending or blocked status, and completion answer separate questions. A milestone is the selected stage or criterion that matters to the present plan, not another kind of task.

candidates.concept: product, output, deliverable, prototype, and workpiece
candidates.placement: Work making, design, task, attempt, and result clauses
candidates.summary: Phi keeps the thing close to the act that made or changed it. A prototype is a trial making under a design; a deliverable is the stated result attached to a task or commitment; and a workpiece is the thing presently being shaped or repaired. Exact product identities remain with their sources.

candidates.concept: maker, craftsperson, repairer, trainer, assessor, inspector, supervisor, and other occasional work roles
candidates.placement: person and role descriptions built from the current work relation
candidates.summary: A relative clause identifies the person performing the present work act. `sithora` records a continuing role when one is actually claimed. This keeps an occasional act from hardening into an occupation or an authority over others.

candidates.concept: workplace, worksite, office, factory, warehouse, and laboratory
candidates.placement: base place vocabulary with Work and Systems activity descriptions
candidates.summary: `keritha` and `phaewi` directly name two stable workspaces. Elsewhere the activity explains the place: writing and coordination can describe an office, making can describe a factory, storage a warehouse, and testing a laboratory. The source keeps any exact institutional or regulated site category.

candidates.concept: repairability, maintainability, durability, reusability, and spare parts
candidates.placement: possibility and condition clauses with Work, Systems, and Ecological vocabulary
candidates.summary: Possibility on `shiroka` or `thiranu` states whether repair or maintenance can be done under the conditions being discussed. Durability follows continued function through time and wear; reuse or recycling states the next act. A spare part is stock held as a component for later use, with its exact identity in the technical record.

candidates.concept: work shifts, breaks, leave, overtime, and working hours
candidates.placement: Work schedules, bounded duration, rest, freedom, and source employment terms
candidates.summary: A shift is a scheduled interval of work; a break is rest within it. Leave adds freedom from the expected work over a stated span, while work beyond the arranged span can be said directly. Exact clock times, contractual categories, entitlements, and payment consequences remain in their records.

candidates.concept: accidents, incidents, near misses, workplace injury, and safe return to use
candidates.placement: intent, danger, consequence, evidence, and safety clauses
candidates.summary: An accident begins with an event that was not intended, then says what changed. A near miss establishes danger without claiming the damage or injury that did not occur. Incident classes and safe-return decisions retain their exact criteria, while Phi records what happened and what the present evidence supports.

candidates.concept: care work, household work, volunteer work, unpaid work, and community labour
candidates.placement: base care and participation vocabulary with Household and Work relations
candidates.summary: The work stays attached to its concrete act, whether care, household effort, repair, or cultivation. `riola` applies when the worker experiences the effort as purposeful contribution; task, workload, drudgery, acceptance, and refusal remain available when that warmer word would hide the relation. Payment status stays with the exact economic record.

candidates.concept: workplace harassment, bullying, discrimination, unsafe pressure, overload, and burnout
candidates.placement: base and module words for conduct, power, bodily effect, access, and redress
candidates.summary: Phi keeps the conduct, power relation, and bodily or participatory effect separate instead of folding them into one workplace label. The account can then state who acted, what happened, how participation changed, and what repair or redress is sought. Exact legal or clinical categories keep their source wording.

candidates.concept: named craft traditions, apprenticeship lineages, community knowledge, technique names, attribution, permissions, and restrictions
candidates.placement: community, cultural, or knowledge-tradition source beside a Work account
candidates.summary: Phi can describe a craft's materials, practice, teaching, and present use. Those broad relations cannot replace the name, history, or authority of the community that carries the knowledge.
candidates.source_rule: Tradition and technique names retain the form their communities and sources use. Lineage, origin, and attribution stay with that account. So do permissions, restrictions, and contested history. Phi may describe learning, making, exchange, or repair around it without claiming that access to a description grants access to the practice.

candidates.concept: patents, copyright, trade marks, trade secrets, intellectual property, and ownership claims over designs or knowledge
candidates.placement: exact legal, institutional, or community source beside Work and Commons relations
candidates.summary: Phi can describe the design, its history, and the claims or restrictions around it. It cannot rebuild the legal or community category that decides what is protected, for how long, or against whom.
candidates.source_rule: Every legal or institutional intellectual-property record retains its exact identity. Its jurisdiction and scope stay with the record. So do the holder, dates, and operative wording. Community knowledge restrictions keep their own authority and terminology. Phi describes the surrounding work and power relations without treating legal ownership as the only possible account.

candidates.concept: sourcing, procurement, logistics, distribution, storage, and disposal
candidates.placement: Work material flow with Ecological, Systems, Commons, and exact economic sources
candidates.summary: Provenance follows where material came from; supplying and transfer follow how it becomes available; stock and inventory separate the shelf from the record. Storage, reuse, recycling, and waste state later stages. Procurement terms that depend on price, contract, or institutional procedure remain with their source.

candidates.concept: quality assurance, quality control, approval, sign-off, warranty, and safe return to use
candidates.placement: Work evaluation with Systems inspection, Commons review and authorization, and exact source records
candidates.summary: The current words already distinguish practical evaluation from institutional claims and present safety. A useful account keeps the criterion and evidence with the person or institution responsible for each one. Exact sign-off, warranty, liability, and compliance terms remain in their operative source.

prompt_sources.note: Used as a cross-linguistic prompt for common concepts, not as a list Phi must lexicalise.

prompt_sources.note: Used to widen the audit beyond the headings that the earlier migration happened to choose.
